data.content:
### Bypass Deployment Protection for a Single Request Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation This method describes how to send a request to your Vercel deployment, overriding any active Deployment Protection (Password Protection, Vercel Authentication, or Trusted IPs) by including a generated automation bypass secret in the request headers or as a query parameter. ```APIDOC ## ANY /your-protected-deployment-path ### Description Send a request to a protected Vercel deployment, bypassing its security measures using a generated automation bypass secret. This can be achieved using either an HTTP header (recommended) or a query parameter. ### Method ANY (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) ### Endpoint /your-protected-deployment-path (Conceptual endpoint representing any path on your Vercel deployment) ### Parameters #### Query Parameters - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The secret generated in your Vercel project settings to bypass deployment protection. Example: `your-generated-secret` #### Request Headers - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The secret generated in your Vercel project settings to bypass deployment protection. This method is strongly recommended over using a query parameter. Example: `your-generated-secret` ### Request Example ```http GET /your-app/dashboard?x-vercel-protection-bypass=your-generated-secret HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app # OR (Recommended using header) GET /your-app/dashboard HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret ``` ### Response #### Success Response (2xx) - **Deployment Content** (html/json/etc.) - The expected content of your deployment as if protection was not active. The specific response structure depends on your application. #### Response Example ```html Your Dashboard

Welcome to your protected application!

You have successfully bypassed protection.

``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Set Vercel Protection Bypass Header for Automation Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation To bypass Vercel protection for automation, specify the `x-vercel-protection-bypass` header or query parameter with your generated secret. This is essential for automation tools that need programmatic access to Vercel deployments. ```HTTP Header x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret (required) ``` -------------------------------- ### Set Bypass Cookie for Follow-up Requests Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation This method allows you to set a cookie that enables subsequent requests from the same client to the deployment to bypass protection without needing to include the `x-vercel-protection-bypass` header or query parameter in every request. This is particularly useful for in-browser testing scenarios. ```APIDOC ## ANY /your-protected-deployment-path ### Description Initiate a request to a protected Vercel deployment that also instructs Vercel to set a bypass cookie. This cookie then allows all subsequent requests from the same client to bypass protection for a limited duration. ### Method ANY (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) ### Endpoint /your-protected-deployment-path (Conceptual endpoint representing any path on your Vercel deployment) ### Parameters #### Query Parameters - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret to bypass initial deployment protection. Example: `your-generated-secret` - **x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie** (string) - Optional - Set to `true` to enable cookie-based bypass with default `SameSite: Lax`. Set to `samesitenone` to use `SameSite: None`, which is required for cross-site contexts like iframes. #### Request Headers - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret to bypass initial deployment protection. (Recommended) Example: `your-generated-secret` - **x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie** (string) - Optional - Set to `true` or `samesitenone` as described for query parameters. (Recommended) ### Request Example ```http GET /your-app/login?x-vercel-protection-bypass=your-generated-secret&x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie=true HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app # OR (Recommended using headers) GET /your-app/login HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: samesitenone ``` ### Response #### Success Response (2xx) - **Deployment Content** (html/json/etc.) - The expected content of your deployment. - **Set-Cookie Header** - An additional `Set-Cookie` header will be present in the response to establish the bypass cookie on the client side. #### Response Example ```http HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/html Set-Cookie: __vercel_deployment_bypass=...; Path=/; Expires=...; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax Login Page

Welcome!

Cookie set for future bypass.

``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Playwright for Vercel Protection Bypass Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation This configuration snippet for Playwright demonstrates how to set HTTP headers to bypass Vercel deployment protection. It uses the `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` environment variable for the bypass secret and optionally sets a cookie for persistent bypass during in-browser testing. ```TypeScript const config: PlaywrightTestConfig = { use: { extraHTTPHeaders: { 'x-vercel-protection-bypass': process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET, 'x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie': true | 'samesitenone' (optional) } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### PATCH /aliases/{id}/protection-bypass Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/aliases/update-the-protection-bypass-for-a-url This endpoint allows you to modify the protection bypass settings for a specific alias or deployment ID. You can set a time-to-live for shareable links, revoke existing links, or create user-scoped protection bypasses. ```APIDOC ## PATCH /aliases/{id}/protection-bypass ### Description Modify the protection bypass settings for a specific alias or deployment. This can include setting time-to-live for shareable links, revoking and regenerating links, or creating user-scoped bypasses. ### Method PATCH ### Endpoint /aliases/{id}/protection-bypass ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **id** (string) - Required - The alias or deployment ID. #### Query Parameters - **teamId** (string) - Optional - The Team identifier to perform the request on behalf of. Example: `team_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l` - **slug** (string) - Optional - The Team slug to perform the request on behalf of. Example: `my-team-url-slug` #### Request Body This endpoint accepts one of the following JSON structures: **Option 1: Modify Shareable Link Properties** - **ttl** (number) - Optional - Time the shareable link is valid for in seconds. If not provided, the link will never expire. Maximum: 63072000. - **revoke** (object) - Optional - Instructions for revoking and regenerating a shareable link. - **secret** (string) - Required - Shareable link to be revoked. - **regenerate** (boolean) - Required - Whether a new shareable link should be created after the provided secret is revoked. **Option 2: Create User Scoped Protection Bypass** - **scope** (object) - Required - Instructions for creating a user scoped protection bypass. - **userId** (string) - Required (if `email` is not provided) - Specified user ID for the scoped bypass. - **email** (string) - Required (if `userId` is not provided) - Specified email for the scoped bypass. - **access** (enum: "denied", "granted") - Required - Invitation status for the user scoped bypass. **Option 3: Override Protection** - **override** (object) - Required - Override protection settings. - **scope** (enum: "alias-protection-override") - Required. - **action** (enum: "create", "revoke") - Required. ### Request Example ```json { "ttl": 123, "revoke": { "secret": "", "regenerate": true } } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - The request was successful. #### Response Example ```json {} ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### PATCH /v1/projects/{idOrName}/protection-bypass Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-protection-bypass-for-automation Updates the protection bypass settings for a specified Vercel project. This endpoint allows you to revoke an existing automation bypass secret, optionally regenerating a new one, or generate an entirely new secret. ```APIDOC ## PATCH /v1/projects/{idOrName}/protection-bypass ### Description Updates the protection bypass settings for a specified Vercel project by either revoking an existing bypass or generating a new one. ### Method PATCH ### Endpoint /v1/projects/{idOrName}/protection-bypass ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **idOrName** (string) - Required - The unique project identifier or the project name #### Query Parameters - **teamId** (string) - Optional - The Team identifier to perform the request on behalf of. - **slug** (string) - Optional - The Team slug to perform the request on behalf of. #### Request Body - **revoke** (object) - Optional - Optional instructions for revoking and regenerating an automation bypass - **secret** (string) - Required - Automation bypass to revoked - **regenerate** (boolean) - Required - Whether or not a new automation bypass should be created after the provided secret is revoked - **generate** (object) - Optional - Generate a new secret. If neither generate or revoke are provided, a new random secret will be generated. - **secret** (string) - Optional - Optional value of the secret to generate, don't send it for oauth2 tokens ### Request Example ```json { "revoke": { "secret": "", "regenerate": true }, "generate": { "secret": "" } } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **protectionBypass** (object) - The updated protection bypass configuration. - **createdAt** (number) - The timestamp when the bypass was created. - **createdBy** (string) - The ID of the user or entity who created the bypass. - **scope** (string) - The scope of the bypass, e.g., "integration-automation-bypass". - **integrationId** (string) - The ID of the integration associated with the bypass. - **configurationId** (string) - The ID of the configuration associated with the bypass. #### Response Example ```json { "protectionBypass": { "createdAt": 1678886400000, "createdBy": "user_id_example", "scope": "integration-automation-bypass", "integrationId": "int_id_example", "configurationId": "conf_id_example" } } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Update Project Protection Bypass using Vercel SDK Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-protection-bypass-for-automation This code snippet demonstrates how to interact with the Vercel API to update a project's protection bypass settings. It shows how to initialize the Vercel SDK with a bearer token and then call the `updateProjectProtectionBypass` method to either revoke an existing bypass, regenerate a new one, or generate a new secret. Dependencies include the Vercel SDK for the respective language and proper authentication. ```go package main\n\nimport(\n\t"os"\n\t"github.com/vercel/vercel"\n\t"context"\n\t"log"\n)\n\nfunc main() {\n s := vercel.New(\n vercel.WithSecurity(os.Getenv("VERCEL_BEARER_TOKEN")), )\n\n ctx := context.Background()\n res, err := s.Projects.UpdateProjectProtectionBypass(ctx, "", nil, nil, nil)\n if err != nil {\n log.Fatal(err)\n }\n if res.Object != nil {\n // handle response\n }\n} ``` ```typescript import { Vercel } from "@vercel/sdk";\n\nconst vercel = new Vercel({\n bearerToken: "",\n});\n\nasync function run() {\n const result = await vercel.projects.updateProjectProtectionBypass({\n idOrName: "",\n teamId: "team_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l",\n slug: "my-team-url-slug",\n requestBody: {},\n });\n\n console.log(result);\n}\n\nrun(); ``` -------------------------------- ### PATCH /v9/projects/{id} - Update Project Password Protection Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/password-protection This endpoint allows you to enable, update, or disable password protection for a specific Vercel project. You can define the scope of protection (e.g., production, all, or preview deployments) and set or remove the password. ```APIDOC ## PATCH /v9/projects/{id} ### Description Updates the password protection settings for an existing Vercel project. This includes enabling, modifying, or disabling password protection for various deployment types. ### Method PATCH ### Endpoint `/v9/projects/{id}` ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **id** (string) - Required - The ID or name of the project to update. #### Request Body - **passwordProtection** (object | null) - Required - An object defining password protection settings, or `null` to disable password protection. - **deploymentType** (string) - Required (if `passwordProtection` is an object) - Specifies which deployments are protected. Allowed values: `prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews` (Standard Protection), `all` (All Deployments), `preview` (Only Preview Deployments). - **password** (string) - Required (if `passwordProtection` is an object) - The password to set for protection. ### Request Example ```json // Enable / Update Password Protection { "passwordProtection": { "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews", "password": "" } } // Disable Password Protection { "passwordProtection": null } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the updated project. - **name** (string) - The name of the updated project. - **passwordProtection** (object | null) - The updated password protection settings for the project, or `null` if disabled. - **deploymentType** (string) - The type of deployments protected. - **protectionDomain** (string) - The domain used for password protection. #### Response Example ```json { "id": "prj_xxxxxxxxxxxx", "name": "my-vercel-project", "passwordProtection": { "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews", "protectionDomain": "my-vercel-project.vercel.app" } } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Vercel Bypass Cookie for Cross-Origin Access Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation If accessing a deployment in a non-direct way, like within an iframe, configure `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie` with the value `samesitenone`. This sets the `SameSite` attribute of the bypass cookie to `None`, facilitating cross-origin cookie usage. ```HTTP Header x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: samesitenone (optional) ``` -------------------------------- ### Patch URL Protection Bypass using Vercel SDK (TypeScript) Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/aliases/update-the-protection-bypass-for-a-url This TypeScript code snippet demonstrates how to use the Vercel SDK to patch the URL protection bypass settings for a specific alias. It requires an alias ID, team ID, and slug, and authenticates using a bearer token for the API request. ```typescript import { Vercel } from "@vercel/sdk"; const vercel = new Vercel({ bearerToken: "", }); async function run() { const result = await vercel.aliases.patchUrlProtectionBypass({ id: "", teamId: "team_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l", slug: "my-team-url-slug", }); console.log(result); } run(); ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Vercel Password Protection via API Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/password-protection This JSON payload is used with the Vercel API to enable, update, or disable password protection for a project. It allows specifying the deployment types to protect ('prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews', 'all', 'preview') and the password. Setting 'passwordProtection' to 'null' disables the feature. ```json // enable / update password protection { "passwordProtection": { "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews" | "all" | "preview", "password": "" } } ``` ```json // disable password protection { "passwordProtection": null } ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Vercel Skew Protection in Other Frameworks (TypeScript/JavaScript) Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/skew-protection For frameworks not natively supported by Vercel's zero-configuration Skew Protection, you can manually append the deployment ID to requests. These examples demonstrate three methods: using a 'dpl' query string parameter, an 'x-deployment-id' HTTP header, or a '__vdpl' cookie. All methods rely on checking `VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED` and using `VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID` environment variables. ```TypeScript const query = process.env.VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED === '1' ? `?dpl=${process.env.VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID}` : ''; const res = await fetch(`/get${query}`); ``` ```TypeScript const headers = process.env.VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED === '1' ? { 'x-deployment-id': process.env.VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID } : {}; const res = await fetch('/get', { headers }); ``` ```TypeScript export default function handler(req, res) { if ( process.env.VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED === '1' && req.headers['sec-fetch-dest'] === 'document' ) { res.setHeader('Set-Cookie', [ `__vdpl=${process.env.VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID}; HttpOnly`, ]); } res.end('

Hello World

'); } ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Vercel Skew Protection with x-deployment-id Header (TypeScript) Source: https://vercel.com/docs/skew-protection This TypeScript code shows how to conditionally add an `x-deployment-id` header to outgoing fetch requests. It relies on the `VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED` environment variable and utilizes `VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID` to direct requests to the correct deployment. This approach is suitable for scenarios where query parameters are not preferred for maintaining Skew Protection. ```typescript const headers = process.env.VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED === '1' ? { 'x-deployment-id': process.env.VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID } : {}; const res = await fetch('/get', { headers }); ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /v1/integrations/deploy/{projectId}/{deployHookId} Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deploy-hooks Triggers a new deployment for a Vercel project using a pre-configured Deploy Hook URL. This allows external systems to initiate builds and deployments without requiring explicit authentication. ```APIDOC ## POST /v1/integrations/deploy/{projectId}/{deployHookId} ### Description Triggers a new deployment for a Vercel project using a Deploy Hook. This endpoint accepts HTTP POST (or GET) requests to initiate a deployment, linked to a specific project, repository, and branch. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /v1/integrations/deploy/{projectId}/{deployHookId} ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **projectId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the Vercel project. - **deployHookId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the Deploy Hook. #### Query Parameters - **buildCache** (boolean) - Optional - If set to `false`, explicitly disables the build cache for the deployment. Default is `true` (build cache is enabled). #### Request Body This endpoint does not require a request body. ### Request Example ``` curl -X POST https://api.vercel.com/v1/integrations/deploy/prj_98g22o5YUFVHlKOzj9vKPTyN2SDG/tKybBxqhQs ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **job.id** (string) - The unique ID of the triggered deployment job. - **job.state** (string) - The current state of the deployment job (e.g., "PENDING"). - **job.createdAt** (number) - Unix timestamp (milliseconds) indicating when the job was created. #### Response Example ```json { "job": { "id": "okzCd50AIap1O31g0gne", "state": "PENDING", "createdAt": 1662825789999 } } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Trigger Vercel Deploy Hook with cURL, disabling Build Cache Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deploy-hooks This `curl` command shows how to trigger a Vercel Deploy Hook while explicitly disabling the build cache for the resulting deployment. Appending `?buildCache=false` to the Deploy Hook URL ensures that the build process starts fresh without utilizing any previously cached artifacts. ```shell curl -X POST https://api.vercel.com/v1/integrations/deploy/prj_98g22o5YUFVHlKOzj9vKPTyN2SDG/tKybBxqhQs?buildCache=false ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Vercel Skew Protection with dpl Query Parameter (TypeScript) Source: https://vercel.com/docs/skew-protection This TypeScript code demonstrates how to conditionally append a `dpl` query parameter to a request URL. It checks for the `VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED` environment variable and uses `VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID` to enable Skew Protection. This method ensures that requests are associated with the correct deployment version for version consistency. ```typescript const query = process.env.VERCEL_SKEW_PROTECTION_ENABLED === '1' ? `?dpl=${process.env.VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_ID}` : ''; const res = await fetch(`/get${query}`); ```
